chapter_context.summary: Presents a song of trust to be sung in Judah, celebrating the strong city of God, the path of the just, the resurrection of the dead, and hiding during indignation.

outline.text: Song of Trust: Perfect Peace for the Mind Stayed on God
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Song of Trust: Perfect Peace for the Mind Stayed on God (26:1-9), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Isaiah.

outline.text: Blindness of the Wicked vs. Glory and Peace of Israel
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Blindness of the Wicked vs. Glory and Peace of Israel (26:10-15), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Isaiah.

outline.text: Israel's Travail, Resurrection of Dead, and Hiding from Indignation
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Israel's Travail, Resurrection of Dead, and Hiding from Indignation (26:16-21), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Isaiah.

key_themes.title: Prophecy of Judgment
key_themes.description: This chapter explores key facets of prophecy of judgment, revealing how God's holy character directs both judgment and redemption.

key_themes.title: The Holy One of Israel
key_themes.description: Isaiah's signature title for God, highlighting His absolute transcendent holiness, moral purity, and unique relationship with His people.

devotional.question: Reflecting on the warnings and promises of Chapter 26, how does God's double character of absolute holiness and deep comfort speak to your life today?
How does this chapter encourage you to trust in God's promises of ultimate renewal and restoration in times of spiritual or physical exile?

cultural_historical.title: The Role of the Prophet in Israel
cultural_historical.text: Prophets in the Hebrew Bible were not just fortune-tellers, but 'covenant enforcement agents'—called to confront sin, demand social justice, and bring hope of future restoration.
